failure_modes.component: spray nozzle — clogging and abrasion wear
failure_modes.symptom: spray flow rate drops > 15% below setpoint; uneven swath distribution visible in coverage map; nozzle output pressure alarm
failure_modes.root_cause: agricultural chemical residue crystallisation inside flat-fan nozzle orifice during drying between missions; abrasive pesticide particulates erode orifice geometry causing atomization degradation

repair_protocol.title: Spray Nozzle Cleaning and Replacement

steps.action: Power off aircraft; release spray system pressure by opening nozzle caps
steps.warning: Never point nozzle at face or unprotected skin — residual chemical pressure

steps.action: Remove nozzle bodies (quarter-turn unlock); soak in clean water for 10 minutes
Use nozzle cleaning brush to clear orifice; backflush with clean water via nozzle cleaning port
steps.tool: nozzle cleaning brush set, syringe with clean water

steps.action: Inspect orifice diameter with nozzle gauge — replace if orifice worn > 5% of nominal diameter
Install new nozzle if worn; quarter-turn lock; verify click-lock engagement
Run spray system test via DJI Agras app — flow rate must be within 5% of target at operating pressure
steps.tool: DJI Agras mobile app, flow rate indicator

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T50 Flat-Fan Nozzle Set (8x)

failure_modes.component: centrifugal pump seal — wear and chemical degradation
failure_modes.symptom: spray system pressure drops below 0.3 MPa; pump motor current rises 20%+ above baseline; chemical leak visible at pump body
failure_modes.root_cause: pump mechanical seal faces erode from abrasive agricultural chemical suspensions; seal material (PTFE or carbon-graphite) requires replacement every 200 operating hours; some pH extremes in fertiliser mixes accelerate degradation

repair_protocol.title: Centrifugal Pump Mechanical Seal Replacement

steps.action: Power off; drain tank and spray lines completely; wear chemical-resistant gloves throughout
steps.warning: Assume all wetted surfaces are contaminated with agricultural chemical — PPE required

steps.action: Remove pump from airframe (4x M5 bolts); collect any residual fluid
Disassemble pump housing (6x M4 screws); expose impeller and seal faces
Remove impeller retaining nut; extract impeller; remove worn seal from shaft
Clean shaft and housing bore with clean water; inspect for shaft scoring
Install new mechanical seal (PTFE-faced); press rotating seat onto shaft to full contact; install stationary seat in housing
Reinstall impeller; torque nut to 8 Nm; reassemble pump housing; reinstall to airframe
Fill tank with clean water; run pump for 3 minutes; inspect for leaks; verify pressure > 0.35 MPa at nominal flow

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T50 Pump Mechanical Seal Kit

failure_modes.component: ESC thermal shutdown — motor overtemperature
failure_modes.symptom: mid-mission ESC over-temperature fault; aircraft reduces speed and altitude; asymmetric motor throttle output; ESC LED flashes red
failure_modes.root_cause: coaxial octorotor ESC operates at sustained high duty cycle during heavy-payload spray missions; thermal interface between ESC PCB and arm tube degrades from vibration-induced fretting; ESC junction temperature exceeds 95C trip threshold

repair_protocol.title: ESC Thermal Interface Refresh and Inspection

steps.action: Power off; remove ESC access cover from arm (2x M3 screws)
Remove ESC from arm tube (4x M3 mounting screws); inspect thermal pad between ESC and arm
Remove degraded thermal pad; clean ESC back and arm contact surface with IPA
Install new 1mm thermal pad (Fujipoly XR-m or equivalent 6 W/mK); press ESC firmly to arm surface; reinstall mounting screws
Inspect ESC for capacitor bulging or PCB burn marks; if present, replace ESC module
Power on; run DJI Agras diagnostic; verify ESC idle temp < 45C at ambient 25C; perform test flight at 80% payload

parts.part_name: Fujipoly XR-m Thermal Gap Pad 50x50x1mm
DJI Agras T50 ESC Module

failure_modes.component: propeller — blade delamination from chemical exposure
failure_modes.symptom: vibration level increases 30%+ above baseline during flight; single propeller RPM drops vs commanded; visual delamination or blistering on prop surface
failure_modes.root_cause: agricultural chemicals (herbicides, fungicides) in aerosol overspray contact propeller blade surface; some chemical combinations attack propeller composite matrix resin causing surface delamination; UV exposure accelerates matrix degradation

repair_protocol.title: Propeller Chemical Damage Inspection and Replacement

steps.action: Pre-flight: inspect all propeller blades under good lighting for surface blistering, delamination, or discoloration; run fingertip across blade surface — any roughness or bubbles = replace
Remove suspect propeller: loosen motor prop lock (counter-clockwise on CW-rotation motors, clockwise on CCW-rotation motors)
Inspect motor shaft for chemical corrosion; clean with IPA if surface oxidation present
Install new propeller; tighten prop lock to click; verify it cannot be pulled off by hand
steps.warning: Never reuse delaminating propellers — blade separation during flight will cause crash under heavy payload

steps.action: Post replacement: run motor run-up test via DJI Agras app; verify vibration < 1.5g RMS on IMU

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T50 Propeller (2x CW + 2x CCW)

failure_modes.component: TB50 intelligent battery — cell imbalance from agricultural heat
failure_modes.symptom: battery remaining capacity shows > 15% drop from last full charge; pre-flight check aborts with battery health warning; battery surface temperature > 50C after charge
failure_modes.root_cause: TB50 batteries stored in direct sun on aircraft between spray passes experience ambient temperatures 50-70C in agricultural settings; accelerated cell degradation from sustained heat during operation; cell group imbalance develops faster than BMS can compensate

repair_protocol.title: TB50 Battery Health Assessment and Replacement

steps.action: Check battery health via DJI Agras app — navigate to Battery > Battery Details; record cycle count and remaining capacity percentage
If remaining capacity < 80% of rated or cycle count > 200: retire battery
Allow battery to cool to < 40C before charging — never charge hot battery; store in shade
Perform 3 full charge-discharge cycles using DJI battery station to recalibrate BMS capacity estimate
If capacity recovers to > 85% after reconditioning, return to service; otherwise replace
Dispose via DJI battery return program; do not puncture or landfill LiPo batteries

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T50 TB50 Intelligent Flight Battery

failure_modes.component: phased array radar — radome contamination
failure_modes.symptom: obstacle avoidance false positives in clean airspace; radar range reduced from 50m to 20m; field map shows phantom obstacles
failure_modes.root_cause: DJI T50 phased array radar uses 77GHz millimeter wave; agricultural chemical residue builds up on radome surface creating dielectric layer that attenuates and reflects radar signal; wet chemical film worse than dry

repair_protocol.title: Radar Radome Chemical Residue Cleaning

steps.action: Power off aircraft; inspect radar radome (lower and forward facing) for chemical film or residue deposits
Dampen microfiber cloth with clean water; gently wipe radome in straight strokes; avoid circular motion
If residue persists, use DJI-approved radome cleaner solution (mild pH 7 aqueous); do not use solvents
steps.warning: Never use acetone, IPA, or agricultural chemical cleaners on radar radome — will etch the radome coating

steps.action: Dry with clean microfiber; power on; run radar self-test via DJI Agras app; verify obstacle detection range > 40m in open field

failure_modes.component: motor bearing — dusty/wet agricultural environment wear
failure_modes.symptom: motor vibration signature changes; individual motor current deviates > 15% from fleet average; motor temperature 10C above peers at same throttle
failure_modes.root_cause: agricultural dust and chemical mist bypass motor can seals over time; bearing contamination accelerates race wear; DJI T50 operates in intensely dusty environments during dry-field applications

repair_protocol.title: Motor Bearing Inspection and Replacement

steps.action: Power off; identify suspect motor by telemetry; remove propeller
Spin motor shaft by hand — feel for roughness, binding, or grinding; any detected: replace motor
Remove motor from arm (4x M3 mounting screws)
Field-level replacement: install new DJI T50 motor; torque M3 screws to 1.5 Nm in star pattern
Reinstall propeller; run motor run-up test; verify vibration < 1.2g RMS

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T50 Motor Assembly

failure_modes.component: flow rate sensor — chemical scale buildup
failure_modes.symptom: flow rate reading drifts > 10% from actual flow; autopilot spray rate varies across field; coverage map shows inconsistent application bands
failure_modes.root_cause: ultrasonic flow sensor exposed to full chemical flow path; dissolved mineral salts and chemical residue crystallise on sensor face and tubing walls; deposits alter acoustic path and reduce measurement accuracy

repair_protocol.title: Flow Rate Sensor Descaling and Calibration

steps.action: Drain spray system; connect clean water to inlet; flush 5 litres through nozzles
Prepare citric acid solution 5% by weight in water; fill tank with 5 litres of solution
steps.tool: citric acid powder, 5L water, measuring scale

steps.action: Run spray pump for 5 minutes; allow solution to soak in lines for 20 minutes; drain and flush with 10 litres clean water
steps.tool: DJI Agras app (manual spray mode), bucket

steps.action: Run flow sensor calibration via DJI Agras app: Maintenance > Spray System > Flow Calibration
steps.tool: DJI Agras app, known-volume collection vessel

steps.action: If calibration error > 10% persists after descaling, replace flow sensor module

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T50 Flow Sensor Module

failure_modes.component: dual pump solenoid valve — sticky valve from crystallised chemical
failure_modes.symptom: one spray boom deactivates mid-mission; flow rate halves; solenoid valve click audible but no flow change; boom isolation fault in app
failure_modes.root_cause: T60 dual-pump system uses solenoid valves to isolate left/right booms; chemical residue crystallises on valve seat after missions without flush; valve actuator cannot overcome crystal buildup even with full solenoid force

repair_protocol.title: Boom Isolation Solenoid Valve Cleaning and Replacement

steps.action: Power off; drain all spray lines; identify stuck solenoid valve from boom isolation fault code
Disconnect valve electrical connector; remove solenoid valve body (2x M4 screws, quick-disconnect fittings)
Soak valve in warm water with 5% citric acid for 30 minutes; actuate manually 20x during soak
Rinse with clean water; test electrical actuation with 12V bench supply — valve must fully open in < 100ms
If valve does not actuate freely after cleaning, replace valve; reinstall with fresh thread seal tape on fittings
Power on; run boom isolation test in DJI Agras app; verify both booms activate and isolate correctly

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T60 Boom Isolation Solenoid Valve

failure_modes.component: 50L folding tank baffle — slosh-induced structural fatigue
failure_modes.symptom: asymmetric yaw during fast direction reversals with partially filled tank; tank audible sloshing during mission; cracked baffle visible on tank inspection
failure_modes.root_cause: T60's 50L tank contains internal baffles to reduce liquid slosh; repeated high-acceleration direction changes at partial fill (25-35L) create hydraulic impact loads on baffle welds exceeding fatigue limit after 500+ full missions

repair_protocol.title: Tank Baffle Inspection and Tank Module Replacement

steps.action: Power off; drain tank completely; remove tank from aircraft (6x M5 quick-release fasteners)
Use inspection camera to view tank interior through fill port; inspect all baffle attachment points for cracks
If baffle cracked at weld: condemn tank module — field repair of HDPE tank not possible; install new tank module
Install new tank; secure all 6 fasteners; reconnect tank level sensor and drain valve
Fill to 25L with clean water; perform direction reversal hover test; verify IMU yaw does not deviate > 3 degrees during reversal

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T60 50L Tank Module

failure_modes.component: ESC gate driver IC — failure from voltage spike
failure_modes.symptom: specific motor drops to 0 RPM mid-flight; ESC error code motor_fault; remaining motors compensate; aircraft descends to emergency landing
failure_modes.root_cause: T60 heavy payload requires high motor current; inductive voltage spikes during rapid throttle changes can exceed gate driver IC breakdown voltage; gate driver ICs in outer arm ESCs experience higher EMI exposure

repair_protocol.title: Failed ESC Module Replacement

steps.action: Power off; identify failed ESC from flight log motor fault codes
Remove propeller from affected motor; remove ESC cover (2x M3 screws)
Remove failed ESC (4x M3 mounting screws); disconnect phase wires, power leads, and CAN connector
Install new ESC module; reconnect phase wires in correct order (label tape reference); reconnect power and CAN
Reinstall ESC cover and propeller; power on; run ESC calibration in DJI Agras app
Perform tethered hover test for 5 minutes; verify all 8 motor currents within 10% of each other
steps.tool: safety tether system, DJI Agras app

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T60 ESC Module

failure_modes.component: boom hinge pin — corrosion seizure
failure_modes.symptom: spray boom will not fold/unfold; hinge pivot galled; boom folds with abnormal force; hinge shows visible rust or galvanic corrosion
failure_modes.root_cause: T60 folding boom hinges are steel pins in aluminium housings; agricultural chemical atmosphere is corrosive; galvanic corrosion at steel/aluminium interface causes hinge seizure when anti-corrosion grease interval is missed

repair_protocol.title: Boom Hinge Pin Lubrication and Replacement

steps.action: Inspect all boom hinges for corrosion and smooth operation before each weekly maintenance
Apply Corrosion-X to hinge pin; work boom through full fold/unfold cycle 10x to distribute lubricant
If hinge still stiff or galled: press out hinge pin using 3mm punch and hammer
Clean hinge bore with spiral brush; coat with Molykote BR-2 grease; install new stainless steel hinge pin
steps.tool: spiral bore brush, Molykote BR-2, bench press or drift

steps.action: Verify boom folds/unfolds with < 5N force; install retention clip

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T60 Boom Hinge Pin (2x stainless)

failure_modes.component: TB60 battery — accelerated aging from partial-state charging
failure_modes.symptom: usable flight time drops below 8 minutes at 60kg payload; app reports cell capacity < 78%; battery swells visibly
failure_modes.root_cause: T60 multi-battery swap operations leave batteries in partial state of charge for hours in hot conditions; repeated partial charge-discharge cycles at high temperature accelerate NMC cell aging through lithium plating at anode

repair_protocol.title: TB60 Battery Reconditioning and Retirement Assessment

steps.action: Check TB60 battery app report: if remaining capacity < 80% or swelling visible, retire immediately
For batteries 80-90% remaining: perform DJI battery station reconditioning cycle (discharge to storage, full charge, discharge to storage)
Measure post-reconditioning capacity — if < 85% of rated, retire
During field operations: avoid storing charged TB60 in direct sun; use insulated battery cases between uses

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T60 TB60 Intelligent Flight Battery

failure_modes.component: RTK antenna — corrosion from chemical spray overspray
failure_modes.symptom: RTK positioning accuracy degrades from 2cm to > 1m; aircraft position hold oscillates; GPS fallback mode triggered
failure_modes.root_cause: RTK antenna on T60 is mounted above tank; spray mist reaches antenna in crosswind conditions; chemical film changes antenna dielectric environment and degrades SMA connector contacts; repeated chemical exposure eventually corrodes connector

repair_protocol.title: RTK Antenna Cleaning and Connector Inspection

steps.action: Power off; inspect RTK antenna patch and SMA connector for chemical residue or corrosion
Clean antenna surface with water-dampened cloth; dry completely
Apply DeoxIT D5 to SMA connector threads; mate and demate 5x; dry with compressed air
Inspect SMA connector center pin for corrosion pitting — if visible green or black corrosion on pin face, replace antenna assembly
Apply thin coat of Corrosion-X to SMA connector exterior after cleaning; do not get inside connector
Power on; check RTK accuracy in open field — verify FIXED solution within 90 seconds and horizontal accuracy < 3cm

parts.part_name: DJI Agras T60 RTK Antenna Assembly

failure_modes.component: gimbal ribbon cable — ZIF connector fatigue
failure_modes.symptom: gimbal image freezes intermittently; camera telemetry disappears from app; physical twisting of gimbal arm resolves temporarily
failure_modes.root_cause: gimbal ribbon cable routes through pivot point and experiences repeated flex cycles; ZIF connector at drone body end develops fretting oxidation from micro-movement during vibration; ribbon cable outer insulation cracks at bend radius

repair_protocol.title: Gimbal Ribbon Cable Replacement

steps.action: Power off; remove gimbal from aircraft (3x M2.5 screws at gimbal mount plate)
Trace ribbon cable from gimbal body to ZIF connector at drone; carefully lift ZIF connector latch with spudger
Extract ribbon cable from ZIF slot; inspect for cracks along flex zone
Slide new ribbon cable into ZIF connector with contacts facing correct direction; press latch down until click
Route cable with same bend radius as original — must not be kinked or pinched by gimbal housing
Reinstall gimbal; power on; verify live video from all cameras; perform gimbal auto-calibration

parts.part_name: DJI M350 RTK Gimbal Ribbon Cable

failure_modes.component: TB65 battery pair — cell capacity mismatch between paired units
failure_modes.symptom: one battery discharges faster than partner; aircraft lands with asymmetric battery SOC (difference > 15%); system logs dual-battery balance warning
failure_modes.root_cause: M350 hot-swap system pairs two TB65 batteries; aging batteries develop differing internal resistance; high-current equalization between mismatched cells stresses the weaker battery and reduces overall capacity

repair_protocol.title: TB65 Battery Pair Evaluation and Matched Replacement

steps.action: Check both TB65 SOH via DJI app; batteries with > 5% SOH difference should not be paired
Perform DJI battery station full discharge-charge cycle on each battery individually; record final capacity
Pair two batteries with capacity within 3% of each other; label pairs with matching tape color
Retire any battery with SOH < 80%

parts.part_name: DJI M350 TB65 Intelligent Flight Battery

failure_modes.component: IP55 ingress protection — gasket degradation
failure_modes.symptom: moisture alarm after rain flight; connector pins show corrosion after wet operations; internal humidity sensor > 70%
failure_modes.root_cause: M350 IP55 protection depends on perimeter gasket and sealed connector boots; repeated battery removal/replacement cycles flex body panels and compress gaskets permanently; field cleaning with pressure washers accelerates gasket degradation

repair_protocol.title: M350 Body Gasket Inspection and Replacement

steps.action: Power off; remove all payloads and batteries; open body panels per DJI M350 service guide (T10 Torx)
Inspect all perimeter gaskets; look for compression set (< 50% original thickness) or tears
Remove degraded gaskets; clean groove with IPA; install new OEM gasket set
Ensure all connector rubber boots are fully seated; replace any cracked boots
Reassemble body panels; torque T10 screws to 1.5 Nm; perform IP55 water spray test 3 minutes at 12.5L/min
steps.tool: torque wrench, garden hose adjustable nozzle

steps.action: Monitor internal humidity via app 30 minutes after spray test — must remain < 60% RH

parts.part_name: DJI M350 RTK Body Gasket Set

failure_modes.component: O3 Enterprise antenna cable — vibration fatigue at connector
failure_modes.symptom: transmission range drops from 20km to < 5km; video latency spikes; connection drops during bank manoeuvres
failure_modes.root_cause: O3 Enterprise antenna cables route through airframe from antenna array to main board; vibration at motor mount frequencies creates resonant stress at SMA connector crimp; repeated flex causes partial centre conductor fracture

repair_protocol.title: O3 Antenna Cable Inspection and Replacement

steps.action: Identify failed antenna direction via DJI RCF signal quality display — weakest direction indicates failed antenna
steps.tool: DJI RC Plus or DJI Pilot 2 app

steps.action: Power off; remove airframe access panel for antenna routing area
Gently flex antenna cable near SMA connector while monitoring cable continuity with DMM — opens indicate fracture location
Disconnect SMA connector; route new OEM cable following original path; reconnect SMA both ends finger-tight + 1/4 turn
steps.warning: Never overtighten SMA — 0.56 Nm maximum; overtorque cracks centre pin

steps.action: Reassemble access panel; perform range test at 2km distance; verify RSSI > -85dBm
steps.tool: DJI Pilot 2 app, open flight area

parts.part_name: DJI M350 O3 Enterprise Antenna Cable Set

failure_modes.component: landing gear strut — damper elastomer fatigue
failure_modes.symptom: hard landings despite slow descent; landing vibration spike > 5g on IMU at touchdown; aircraft bounces on landing; damper visually compressed or cracked
failure_modes.root_cause: M350 landing gear uses elastomer vibration dampers; repeated hard landings or landing on uneven terrain compress dampers beyond design limits; UV and ozone exposure accelerates elastomer oxidation and cracking

repair_protocol.title: Landing Gear Damper Replacement

steps.action: Inspect all 4 landing gear dampers; press each with thumb — damper should spring back; cracked or permanently compressed: replace
Remove landing gear strut from body (2x M3 screws per strut); extract damper insert from strut tip
Press new damper insert into strut tip until seated; reinstall strut; torque M3 screws to 1.5 Nm
Perform 5 practice landings on flat surface; verify IMU touchdown vibration < 3g

parts.part_name: DJI M350 RTK Landing Gear Damper Set (4x)

predictive_signals.signal_source: IMU flight log at touchdown event

failure_modes.component: RTK antenna — ground plane corrosion
failure_modes.symptom: RTK fix takes > 3 minutes; horizontal accuracy degrades to > 10cm in good GNSS conditions; position hold oscillates
failure_modes.root_cause: M350 RTK antenna ground plane is aluminium; field exposure to rain, dew, and cleaning agents causes surface oxidation; oxidised ground plane reduces antenna radiation efficiency and multi-path rejection

repair_protocol.title: RTK Antenna Ground Plane Cleaning and Inspection

steps.action: Power off; inspect RTK antenna and ground plane for oxidation (white powder on aluminium)
Clean ground plane with aluminium-safe contact cleaner; remove white oxide deposits
Apply thin coat of ACF-50 anti-corrosion film to ground plane; allow 5 minutes to dry
Check SMA connector on RTK module for corrosion; treat with DeoxIT if needed
Power on in open sky; verify RTK FIXED solution < 90 seconds and accuracy < 3cm CEP

parts.part_name: DJI M350 RTK Antenna Assembly (if ground plane damaged)

failure_modes.component: obstacle avoidance cameras — contamination and calibration drift
failure_modes.symptom: obstacle avoidance system switches to degraded mode; drone slows and widens clearance margins; false collision warnings in open airspace
failure_modes.root_cause: Skydio X10 uses 6 fisheye cameras for 360-degree obstacle avoidance; dust, pollen, or water droplets on camera windows degrade AI confidence scores; thermal shock from indoor-to-outdoor transitions causes lens focus drift

repair_protocol.title: Obstacle Avoidance Camera Cleaning and Recalibration

steps.action: Power off; inspect all 6 camera windows under oblique light for dust, smudges, or water spots
Blow loose dust with sensor-safe air blower; never use compressed air cans (propellant can freeze lens coating)
Wipe each camera window with dry optical microfiber cloth in straight strokes; use fresh section for each window
If smudge persists: one drop lens cleaning solution on cloth only (never on lens); wipe; dry with fresh cloth section
Power on; allow 10 minutes temperature equilibration; run Skydio obstacle avoidance calibration via Skydio app
steps.tool: Skydio app, open area with known objects at 5m

steps.action: Verify obstacle detection range > 10m in all quadrants with 1x1m test panel

failure_modes.component: Qualcomm SoC — thermal throttling from blocked vent
failure_modes.symptom: AI inference latency increases; obstacle detection response time > 200ms; drone reduces maximum speed autonomously; SoC temperature alarm
failure_modes.root_cause: X10 relies on passive convection through body vents for SoC thermal management; fine dust accumulates in vent mesh reducing airflow; SoC thermal throttles at 85C junction temperature

repair_protocol.title: Body Vent Cleaning for SoC Thermal Management

steps.action: Power off; identify vent mesh locations on drone body (typically 4 vent slots on lower body)
Use sensor-safe blower to blow dust out from inside outward via battery bay access (blow into body, out through vents)
Use soft horsehair brush to remove any remaining debris from vent mesh
Power on; perform 10-minute flight; verify SoC temperature stays < 78C via Skydio flight data export

failure_modes.component: motor bearing — pitting from grit contamination
failure_modes.symptom: motor vibration signature changes; one motor RPM differs from commanded by > 5%; audible grinding under load; motor current 15%+ above peers
failure_modes.root_cause: X10 IP55 motor shaft seals degrade at 18-24 months; fine silica dust from construction inspection or agricultural environments enters motor and causes bearing race pitting; bearing noise often only detectable at high RPM

repair_protocol.title: Motor Bearing Inspection and Motor Replacement

steps.action: Power off; remove propeller (3x M2 screws)
Spin motor shaft by hand at 1 revolution per second; feel for roughness or grinding; even slight roughness = replace motor
Remove motor from arm (3x M2 mounting screws from inside arm tube); disconnect 3-phase wire connector
Install new motor; reconnect 3-phase wire connector (orientation must match — verify in Skydio service manual)
steps.warning: Phase wire reversal will cause motor to spin wrong direction — verify connector orientation before powering on

steps.action: Reinstall propeller; power on; run motor self-test via Skydio Enterprise Dashboard; verify vibration < 0.8g RMS

parts.part_name: Skydio X10 Motor Assembly

failure_modes.component: thermal IR payload microbolometer — pixel dropout
failure_modes.symptom: thermal image shows fixed dark or bright pixels in same location frame-to-frame; cold spots pattern in uniform-temperature target; thermal measurement accuracy degrades
failure_modes.root_cause: uncooled microbolometer detector has pixel non-uniformity that increases with thermal cycling; hot-pixel count increases after 5000+ flight hours; periodic flat field correction (FFC) can compensate but cannot recover pixels with > 50% response loss

repair_protocol.title: Thermal Payload FFC Calibration and Sensor Assessment

steps.action: Run flat field correction: in Skydio app, navigate to Thermal Settings > Calibration > Run FFC; allow 30 seconds
Capture thermal image of uniformly-heated blackbody or flat wall > 2m from drone; count hot/cold pixel clusters
If < 10 fixed pixels: continue service after FFC recalibration
If > 50 fixed pixels or > 1% of frame: escalate to Skydio Enterprise Support for sensor replacement — not field-serviceable

tools_required: uniform flat surface for thermal reference

failure_modes.component: LiHV battery — cold weather sudden voltage collapse
failure_modes.symptom: battery voltage drops sharply during high-throttle manoeuvre in ambient < 5C; drone enters forced landing; post-flight battery shows < 20% SOC despite pre-flight indication of 60%+
failure_modes.root_cause: LiHV chemistry used in X10 battery has higher nominal voltage (4.35V/cell) but poor low-temperature performance; internal resistance rises 3-4x below 5C; rapid throttle demand causes voltage sag below cutoff threshold triggering emergency land

repair_protocol.title: Cold Weather Battery Pre-Warming Protocol

steps.action: In ambient < 10C, warm battery to minimum 15C before flight: insert battery in drone and idle aircraft (motors off) for 5 minutes with aircraft powered on; battery self-heating from electronics sufficient
Verify battery temperature via Skydio app Battery Health screen shows > 15C before takeoff
Reduce maximum speed setting to 80% in cold conditions; reduce mission duration to 70% of nominal endurance
Store batteries at room temperature between flights — never store cold batteries charged

failure_modes.component: IP55 port covers — loss from repeated access
failure_modes.symptom: USB-C and microSD port covers missing; water ingress risk through open ports during IP55-rated operations; moisture detected in storage port area
failure_modes.root_cause: Skydio X10 port covers use thin silicone rubber attached to body; repeated port access for data download detaches cover hinge; covers frequently lost in field; once lost, IP55 rating at those ports is void

repair_protocol.title: Port Cover Replacement and Water Ingress Prevention

steps.action: Inspect all port covers (USB-C, microSD, battery contact) before each mission for presence and seating
Replace missing port covers from spares kit; press firmly until click
If hinge attachment point is damaged, apply thin bead of Loctite 401 to hinge attachment point; allow 5 minutes cure
Never fly in rain without all port covers installed and confirmed seated

tools_required: Skydio X10 port cover spares kit

parts.part_name: Skydio X10 Port Cover Set

suppliers.name: Skydio Enterprise Support
suppliers.notes: Skydio hardware is US-controlled and has export restrictions

failure_modes.component: servo actuator — elevator/aileron gear train wear
failure_modes.symptom: control surface has play > 2 degrees; autopilot reports control surface limit reached during routine manoeuvre; aircraft oscillates in pitch or roll
failure_modes.root_cause: servo output gear train uses plastic final stage gears (weight optimisation); repeated high-speed flight with gusty conditions causes gear tooth wear; aileron servos see highest duty cycle in turbulence

repair_protocol.title: Control Surface Servo Gear Inspection and Replacement

steps.action: With aircraft powered off, manually deflect each control surface; any perceptible freeplay = servo requires replacement
Measure freeplay with protractor at control surface — > 1.5 degrees requires immediate servo replacement
Remove wing panel per Zipline service procedure; expose servo bay
Disconnect servo connector; remove servo from bay (3x M2.5 screws); disconnect control horn
Install new servo; reconnect control horn at same hole position; connect servo connector
Run Zipline GCS control surface calibration; verify deflection vs command within 0.5 degrees across full travel

parts.part_name: Zipline P2 Control Servo (field replacement unit)

failure_modes.component: pusher propeller — delamination from UV and fatigue
failure_modes.symptom: vibration increase > 50% on IMU at cruise speed; aircraft speed reduced by autopilot; visible surface delamination on prop blade
failure_modes.root_cause: P2 pusher prop is CFRP/fiberglass composite; UV exposure from equatorial deployment sites (Ghana, Rwanda, Nigeria) accelerates matrix resin degradation; high-cycle fatigue from 34m/s cruise and repeated catapult launches

repair_protocol.title: Pusher Propeller Inspection and Replacement

steps.action: Pre-flight mandatory: inspect propeller under bright light for delamination blistering or any visible damage; feel for surface roughness
Tap prop blade with fingernail along span — hollow sound indicates subsurface delamination; reject any blade with hollow tap response
Remove propeller spinner (3x M3 screws); remove propeller nut (CCW thread — turn clockwise to loosen)
steps.tool: JIS #1 screwdriver, 13mm socket, thread direction warning

steps.action: Install new prop; tighten nut to 12 Nm; reinstall spinner
Perform ground run-up at 75% power for 30 seconds; verify IMU vibration < baseline +20%

parts.part_name: Zipline P2 Pusher Propeller

failure_modes.component: pitot tube — blockage from insects or moisture
failure_modes.symptom: airspeed indication erratic; autopilot enters degraded mode; airspeed reads 0 or > 60m/s at known cruise speed
failure_modes.root_cause: pitot tube open to airstream for airspeed measurement; small insects (wasps, spiders) create nests inside pitot tube during overnight storage; equatorial humidity causes moisture condensation blockage

repair_protocol.title: Pitot Tube Inspection and Clearing

steps.action: Pre-flight mandatory: visually inspect pitot tube opening; use flashlight to verify clear opening; no nest material visible
Blow through pitot tube gently with mouth from aircraft end to clear; air must flow freely through both static and total pressure ports
steps.warning: Never use compressed air or sharp objects to clear pitot — can damage pressure sensor diaphragm

steps.action: If blockage persists, use pipe cleaner of appropriate diameter to gently clear tube
After clearing, blow through again to confirm clear passage; perform airspeed calibration ground test
Preventive: install pitot cover during all ground operations and overnight storage; remove cover as part of pre-flight checklist

parts.part_name: Pitot Tube Fabric Cover (pack of 10)

failure_modes.component: catapult/arrestor system — fatigue at launch bracket
failure_modes.symptom: launch velocity varies > 10% between launches; aircraft enters runway faster than expected; launch abort triggered by autopilot velocity check
failure_modes.root_cause: pneumatic catapult launch system imparts 15G+ to aircraft at launch; catapult attachment bracket on aircraft fuselage experiences repeated high impulse loads; bracket-to-fuselage bond fatigues at composite interface

repair_protocol.title: Catapult Bracket Bond Inspection and Recertification

steps.action: After every 500 launches: inspect catapult attachment bracket for visible cracks in surrounding composite or bracket edge separation
steps.tool: magnifying glass, flashlight, coin tap test

steps.action: Tap coin around bracket perimeter — hollow sound indicates delamination; any delamination = aircraft grounded
If delamination detected: escalate to Zipline aircraft depot for structural recertification — not field repairable
steps.warning: Field repair of load-bearing composite structures requires vacuum bagging and controlled cure — do not attempt field repair

steps.action: Inspect catapult pneumatic line fitting for cracks or O-ring compression set; replace O-ring if needed
Log launch velocities from last 10 launches; if standard deviation > 1m/s, inspect pneumatic system for leaks

parts.part_name: Catapult Pneumatic Line O-Ring Kit

failure_modes.component: LiPo flight battery — capacity fade from high-rate cycling
failure_modes.symptom: flight range drops below 25km; emergency reserve lands at 0% vs 10% expected; battery discharge curve shows flat section indicating dead cell group
failure_modes.root_cause: P2 catapult launch requires 10C+ discharge rate instantaneously; repeated high-C discharge cycles accelerate LiPo capacity fade; equatorial high-temperature storage between flights further degrades cell chemistry

repair_protocol.title: Flight Battery Capacity Assessment and Replacement

steps.action: Log flight range from last 5 missions; if average < 28km, perform battery capacity test
Perform battery capacity discharge test via Zipline battery tester: full charge to 4.20V/cell; discharge at 2C to 3.50V/cell; record Ah
If measured capacity < 85% of rated: retire battery; log serial number and cycle count
Install new battery; perform 3 break-in charge-discharge cycles at 1C before high-rate service

parts.part_name: Zipline P2 Flight LiPo Battery Pack

failure_modes.component: parachute delivery pod — deployment failure
failure_modes.symptom: payload pod does not separate from aircraft at delivery point; delivery audit shows package not dropped; aircraft returns with payload
failure_modes.root_cause: pod release mechanism uses pyrotechnic squib or servo-actuated release; squib moisture degradation in humid equatorial climates; servo release linkage corrosion from humidity

repair_protocol.title: Delivery Pod Release Mechanism Inspection and Service

steps.action: Inspect pod release servo linkage monthly for corrosion; lubricate all pivot points with Corrosion-X
Test release mechanism with inert training pod: command release from GCS; verify pod separates within 100ms of command
steps.tool: Zipline GCS, inert training pod, timer

steps.action: If release fails: remove pod bay cover; inspect servo arm and release pin for corrosion or binding
If servo arm corroded: replace servo module (full FRU swap); if release pin bent: replace pin
If pyrotechnic squib system: check squib date code — replace annually regardless of use; store squibs in dry sealed container
steps.tool: squib date code label, sealed dry container
steps.warning: Pyrotechnic squib handling requires operator certification — never disassemble or probe live squib

parts.part_name: Zipline P2 Release Servo Module

failure_modes.component: pitot heater — open circuit failure in cold elevation deployments
failure_modes.symptom: pitot tube icing in missions above 2500m altitude or ambient < 2C; airspeed reads zero after 5 minutes at altitude; autopilot enters airspeed-unavailable mode
failure_modes.root_cause: P2 pitot heater is thin-film resistive element; vibration-induced fatigue crack in heater lead wire causes open circuit; heater circuit not monitored in real-time — failure only apparent when icing occurs

repair_protocol.title: Pitot Heater Circuit Continuity Test and Repair

steps.action: Monthly maintenance: with aircraft powered off, measure pitot heater resistance at aircraft connector — expect 8-12 ohms; open circuit (>1Mohm) = heater failed
If open circuit: remove nose fairing (6x T8 screws); trace heater wire to fracture point
Repair wire fracture with solder splice; insulate with dual-wall heat shrink; ensure strain relief at pitot mount
steps.tool: soldering iron, dual-wall heat shrink, heat gun

steps.action: Verify heater resistance restores to 8-12 ohms; reinstall fairing
Test heater function: power on aircraft; enable pitot heat from GCS; verify pitot tube surface reaches 40C within 60 seconds via IR thermometer

parts.part_name: Pitot Heater Wire Repair Kit (22AWG silicone wire + heat shrink)

suppliers.name: Zipline Field Service
suppliers.notes: Zipline operates closed service model — all parts through Zipline directly; no third-party parts supported
